title: What is a cell type and how to define it?
abstract: Proposes a framework for defining cell types using multimodal data. Argues that cell types should be defined by convergent evidence from transcriptomic, epigenomic, morphological, electrophysiological, and connectomic data, and discusses how single-cell genomics is transforming our understanding of cellular diversity in the brain.
notes: New paper. Conceptual framework for cell type definition using multimodal data.
